criteria.name: Checklist Coverage
criteria.description: Does the evaluation address all 8 cognitive design dimensions (visibility, hierarchy, chunking, simplicity, memory, feedback, consistency, scanning)?

scores.1: Evaluates fewer than 3 dimensions - major gaps in assessment
scores.3: Evaluates 5-6 dimensions - core areas covered, some gaps
scores.5: All 8 dimensions evaluated with specific findings for each

criteria.name: Visualization Audit Rigor
criteria.description: Are all 4 audit criteria (clarity, efficiency, integrity, aesthetics) scored with evidence?

scores.1: No structured scoring - subjective opinions without framework
scores.2: Partial scoring - some criteria addressed without evidence
scores.3: All 4 criteria scored but evidence thin for some
scores.4: All 4 criteria scored with supporting evidence
scores.5: All 4 criteria scored with detailed evidence, test results, and specific examples

criteria.name: Severity Classification
criteria.description: Are findings properly classified by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)?

scores.1: No severity classification - flat list of issues
scores.2: Inconsistent severity - integrity issues not flagged as critical
scores.3: Generally correct severity levels with minor misclassifications
scores.4: Accurate severity with proper prioritization (integrity first)
scores.5: Precise severity with clear justification for each classification

criteria.name: Fix Recommendations
criteria.description: Are specific, actionable fixes provided for each finding?

scores.1: No fixes provided - problems identified without solutions
scores.2: Vague fixes - general suggestions without specifics
scores.3: Adequate fixes - actionable for most findings
scores.4: Good fixes - specific and prioritized with effort estimates
scores.5: Excellent fixes - specific, prioritized, with before/after examples

criteria.name: Evidence Quality
criteria.description: Are findings supported by specific observations rather than general impressions?

scores.1: No evidence - assertions without supporting observations
scores.2: Weak evidence - vague references to issues
scores.3: Adequate evidence - most findings have specific observations
scores.4: Strong evidence - all findings cite specific design elements
scores.5: Excellent evidence - specific elements cited with cognitive impact explained

criteria.name: Cognitive Grounding
criteria.description: Are evaluation criteria grounded in cognitive science principles (not just aesthetic preference)?

scores.1: Pure aesthetics - no cognitive science basis
scores.2: Limited grounding - occasional reference to principles
scores.3: Adequate grounding - main findings connected to cognitive principles
scores.4: Good grounding - findings consistently tied to perception, memory, or attention research
scores.5: Excellent grounding - every finding connected to specific cognitive mechanism with WHY explanation

criteria.name: Completeness
criteria.description: Does the evaluation cover the full design artifact without overlooking sections?

scores.1: Evaluated only a fragment - major sections ignored
scores.2: Partial evaluation - some charts or sections skipped
scores.3: Most of the design evaluated - minor areas missed
scores.4: Complete evaluation of all visible design elements
scores.5: Comprehensive evaluation including edge cases, responsive views, and interaction states

passing_note: Average score must be >= 3.5 for evaluation to be considered thorough. Scores below 3 in Severity Classification or Evidence Quality require revision.
